long_desc: A key of dark black metal is here.
A black key of very hard metal lies here.
A key graven with evil runes lies in the dust.
A key of pale-green oxidized copper lies here.
A key of dull iron lies in the dirt.
A small, black skeleton key lies in the dirt.
A bronze key with intricate wards lies here.
A huge key, made of black iron, lies here.
A small key of blued steel lies here.
A red-colored steel key lies unnoticed in the dust.
A golden key brazed to a fine gold ring lies here.
An ornate silver key lies on the ground, unwanted.
An iron key has been copper plated to keep off rust.
A large wedge of cheddar cheese gathers a lovely mold here.
This bright iron key can open a lock, somewhere.
A large lumpy bottle of some strange brew is here.
A bronze chest, made ugly with grotesque bas-reliefs, stands here.
A bottle of oily black liquid lies on its side.

extra_descs.desc:
As you turn the crown over in your hands, you feel twice the warrior you used
to be, ready even to lead an army into battle.  Surely no magic can harm such a
great one as wears this crown.

long_desc: The crown of the Lonoyan king rolls by and falls flat with a clang.

extra_descs.desc:
It is sealed with a metal cap.  You KNOW this stuff is bad,bad,bad.  It is
probably not single-malt.  More likely eight malt.

long_desc: A bottle of Inver House blended whiskey lies here.

extra_descs.desc:
It is the famous Polish hunters' stew that was immortalized in Adam
Mickiewicz' epic poem Pan Tadeusz.  Venison and other meats are boiled with
kapusta (sauerkraut) to make a delicious dinner, fit for King Korbor of Lonoya.

long_desc: A steaming bowl of Polish hunters' stew has been left here.
A lovely yellow apple with gold flecks and red cheeks lies here.
The squirrels have left a few nuts here.
A Lonoyan man's shirt lies in a crumpled heap.
A Lonoyan woman's shortgown hangs forgotten on a nearby peg.
Some Lonoyan left in such a hurry, he forgot his breeches.
A skirt lies here.
A pair of clodhoppers lies here.
A kerchief lies here.
A large red neckerchief is draped over a stone.
A claymore lies here.

extra_descs.desc: The hilt is engraved with one word, a name ;it is the name "MacLeod".

long_desc: A beautiful claymore with an engraved hilt lies here.
A fifteen-foot spear is hung on the wall here.
A hand axe lies here.
A battle axe lies here.
A long, single-edged knife lies here.
A quarterstaff lies here.
A broadsword lies here.
A shirt of chain mail lies in a heap.
An open-faced helm lies on its side.
A pile of forest green cloth lies in a crumpled heap.
A pile of red cloth lies on the ground.
A pile of blue cloth lies on the ground.
A pile of green cloth lies on the ground.
A backpack lies here.
A glass flask lies here.
A water skin lies here
A many-pocketed robe lies here.

extra_descs.desc:
This robe is worn by only the most prestigious.  An aura of peace and
contentment surrounds the robe.  Making it feel almost other worldly.  The
fabric is soft and very pliable.  This robe would be very comfortable to wear.

long_desc: A white robe lies here.
A medium-sized wooden shield lies in the dust.
A short bow lies here unstrung, its string wrapt round its stave.
A breastplate made of leather hardened in hot oil lies here.
A leather jacket studded with metal plates lies in a heap.

extra_descs.desc:
It is engraved with these arcane runes:

                      judicandus dies

long_desc: A small flask full of an aromatic, and alcoholic, fluid.
A small flask of clear, glimmering liquid.
A small flask of fragrant golden liquid.
A large vial of bubbling red fluid lies here, firmly capped.
A scroll labelled "To Augment Thy Craftsmanship" lies here.
A scroll labelled "To Bring the Spring Rains" lies here.
A scroll labelled "To Increase Thy Fighting Spirit" lies here.

extra_descs.desc:
The Legend of Nambor.
    In days now forgotten to any but the living God, there lived a
    valiant Man, Nambor by name, who dared enter the evil tunnels
    in the bowels of the mountains.  From Symtoto he came, through
    paths untrodden, until after great struggle he found a shaft
    leading down into deep darkness.  But that was the start of poor
    Nambor's trials, for he found the entrance was too high to leave
    through. Now must he roam the tunnels of the evil creatures that
    gnaw the rocks of the Underdark, till God shows him an escape
    from the tunnels, or from this world.
    And bravely he went forth, ever climbing deeper, sword clutched
    in hand.  And 'tis said by the elves that he saw there many a
    marvel; rocks that turned into vile beasts when touched, and
    unseen creatures that lurked in the shadows.  And some there are
    that say he escaped at last, but others say he never left, but
    died in the dark, broken sword still in his hand.
    And now these tunnels are full of goblins, yet still some of the
    nameless things Nambor fought remain.  Where now is one willing
    to enter and discover the ancient hero's fate? Where now is the
    hero, to save Lonoya from the goblins?
                                                     Cwell the Bard

long_desc: A scroll labelled "The Legend of Nambor" lies here.
A fine gold ring set with an opal lies on the ground.
A huge pile of gold is here.

extra_descs.desc: It is a hideous, leering goblin head carved in bone.

long_desc: A jagged black knife with a goblin-head carved on its hilt lies here.
A blackened scimitar engraved with evil runes lies here.
A large human bone set with cruel jagged spikes lies here.
A shortbow of ogre horn backed with sinew lies here.
A short spear with a broad blade lies here.
A jagged, black, rune-carved poleaxe is stuck into the ground.
A hauberk of mithril rings lies here.
An elvish bow of aurodiea wood lies here.

extra_descs.desc:
The mark "D.  F.  " with a stamped-in hammer is on its hilt, and the blade
bears the name "Krat".  Perhaps it is magic?  But its blade, at least, is very
well-tempered, and its edge razor-sharp.

long_desc: A finely crafted broadsword must have been plundered from Lonoya.
Some sturdy but ugly platemail has been painted over red. Doesn't help.
A huge black club with metal bands lies here.

extra_descs.desc:
It is in orcish:
                     I AM TOADSTOOL THE MIGHTY
                       NONE SHALL DEFEAT ME

long_desc: An iron collar lies in the dirt. It has some writing on it.
A severed goblin head lies here.  Looks tasty.
The hide of a goblin lies on the ground, smelling very bad.
A frilly pink apron, stained with blood, is on the ground.
A horrible jagged scimitar of orcish make is stuck into the ground.
A gigantic mountain of plunder is piled in every corner.
A crude wooden chest sits here.
An ebony chest sits here.
A light chest of pine sits here.
A mining pick lies here, discarded.
A mining pick lies here, discarded.

extra_descs.desc: It doesn't look too good.  I wouldn't drink it if I were you!

long_desc: A tub of dirty water for quenching tools sits here.
You stand by the ancient Well of Jakur, delved thousands of years ago.

extra_descs.desc: The fountain is filled with cheap, but not sour, wine.

long_desc: A magical fountain of marble spouts the blood-red wine.

extra_descs.desc:
It is an oil-on-wood portrait of the good King.  No, you can't steal it, my
friend.  It is, strange to say, firmly bolted to the wall, not hung from the
usual nail.

long_desc: A portrait of King Korbor in robes of state hangs on the wall.
Some waybread has been placed here.
